title: object-removal guard (compile error + message)
manager: A guard that turns "you removed a field or state something still depends on" into a compile error with a message naming what broke, instead of a runtime `undefined` surfacing three steps downstream. This is the safety net that makes refactoring a machine's data shape safe: delete something still in use and the compiler stops you at the edit, not the user at runtime with a mystery failure.
cs: compile-time removal guard; the diagnostic names the dependent site; part of the object-removal safety story (§4.2).
